spec.reasoning: Risk flags indicate the task inserts/deletes rows, references absolute row numbers (A7:A1000), and depends on blank row positions. Therefore, inserting blank rows is extremely dangerous — it would shift data and break the hardcoded range reference in the instruction, and any extra blank rows could interfere with the macro's row-insertion logic. I skip blank_row_insert entirely. Instead I use: (1) Column renames — all headers are renamed at the same descriptiveness level (full names to full names, abbreviations to abbreviations like 'Quarter 4' → 'Q4'). None of these column names appear as general English words in the instruction. (2) Sheet rename — 'Before Insert Row' → 'Pre Row Insert' at the same descriptiveness level. (3) Distractor column — 'Avg Ships Per Qtr' is plausibly related to the shipping data but clearly distinct from existing columns. This tests whether the agent hardcodes column count. The instruction references Column A and range A7:A1000 which remain unchanged, preserving task logic.
